data.body:
8d36 VALIDATION COMPLETE (held-out oracle = real commands + CI).
RED→GREEN (coverage-combine, the key AC):
- RED: -n4 --cov WITHOUT parallel=true → coverage collapses (full suite 54%, subset 9.69%) << fail_under=70 (FAIL) + leaks .coverage worker files into checkout, which trip the suite's own repo-root leak-guard (3 spurious test errors).
- ROOT CAUSE found during GREEN: under -n4, coverage worker files (.coverage.<host>.pid…) land in repo root; a still-running worker's per-test leak-guard sees a finished worker's file as a NEW entry and DELETES it → corrupts combine (54%) + fails ~3 tests. Fixed by teaching the leak-guard (tests/conftest.py _no_repo_root_leaks) to ignore coverage.py artifacts (.coverage/.coverage.*/coverage.xml) — they are plugin-produced + gitignored, not test leaks. Root-cause fix works for CI AND the documented local -n4 --cov command.
- GREEN: WITH parallel=true + leak-guard fix → full default suite -n4 --dist worksteal: TOTAL 79.94% (≥70), 5884 passed / 16 skipped / 0 failed / 0 errors, ONLY combined .coverage remains (no .coverage.* leftover). Teeth: removing parallel=true demonstrably reverts to 54%.
Integration tier: -n4 --dist loadgroup → 75 passed; live tests confined to xdist_group('live_reconcile_e2e') (verified via @-suffix). The 1 live failure (test_delete_permission_probe 'never became index-visible') is a PRE-EXISTING live-Jira eventual-consistency flake — reproduced SERIALLY (no xdist) too (REB-1107) → NOT caused by my change; self-skips in CI.
Local-only artifact excluded: test_resolve_symbol_confirms_third_party_module fails on this worktree because .venv is nested in the repo (yaml classifies as repo-local not third-party); fails serially on this tree, passes in CI. Deselected from local pre-flight.
Files changed for 8d36: pyproject.toml (parallel=true + pytest-xdist==3.8.0 + pytest-split==0.11.0), .github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml + test.yml (default -n<cores> worksteal, integration -n<cores> loadgroup), tests/integration/test_reconcile_live_e2e.py (xdist_group mark), tests/conftest.py (leak-guard ignores coverage artifacts), docs/coverage.md (xdist section). DISCOVERED: pre-existing live-suite flake — will file discovered_from.
